Telemetry Attribution Fix

PR #2850 — restore skill-usage.jsonl + agent-type attribution at SubagentStop. Pick a spawn kind to see how the type resolves, before vs after.

Defect 1 · agent-type attribution

Simulate a SubagentStop payload
SubagentStart — stages agent_id → type → session-state.json — read at → SubagentStop

Payload seen by trackAgentResult()


    

      

        
Before (main)

        

        

      

      

        
After (this PR)

        

        

      

    

  


  
Defect 2 · skill-usage.jsonl restored

  

    
The #959 hooks.json rework dropped the skillTracker dispatch; #2813 restored the dispatch but not the user-scoped write. The file has been dead since 2026-06-21 while the HTTP sink still allowlists it (OrkSkillUsage) and /ork:analytics still reads it.

    

      

        
Before — writers in skillTracker

        
skill-usage.log        (project-local)
skill-channels.jsonl   (project-local)
sessions.db            (project-local)
✗ skill-usage.jsonl   (user-scoped) — DROPPED

      

      

        
After — restored write

        
appendAnalytics('skill-usage.jsonl', {
  ts, pid, skill,
  ...getTeamContext()   // team?
})

        
Schema { ts, pid, skill, team? } — matches skills/analytics/data-locations.md + the sink allowlist.

      

    

  


  
Why SubagentStart, not PostToolUse[Agent]

  

    
The forensic brief suggested capturing at PostToolUse[Agent]. That fires after SubagentStop for foreground agents — too late for the stop writer. SubagentStart fires before SubagentStop for both foreground and background agents, and already stages agent_id.

    

      foregroundPreToolUse → SubagentStart → run → SubagentStop → PostToolUse[Agent]
      backgroundPreToolUse → PostToolUse[Agent] → SubagentStart → run → SubagentStop
    

    
SubagentStart precedes SubagentStop in both — the single correct capture point.
